title: Non-record- QAT cooldown + INT4 MLP + NuMuon-lite - 1.12 BPB
author: marinabar
body:
This PR builds on the current SOTA with three techniques for optimizing information density.
Results: 1.12 val BPB, 2.9 validation loss (non-record).

**1. QAT-Fused Cooldown**
Rather than running GPTQ after training ends, we enable INT6 fake-quantization during the LR warmdown so the optimizer can actively adapt weights to quantization noise while it's still learning. In this small setup post-hoc quantization accumulates a lot of error, so fusing QAT into the cooldown phase avoids this cleanly.

**2. INT4 MLP + INT6 Attention Mixed Precision**
MLP weights are the largest and most redundant part of the model, so we drop them to INT4 while keeping attention at INT6. This frees ~2.9MB which goes back into model capacity. The quality is much better than post-hoc INT4 thanks to the QAT cooldown.

**3. NuMuon-lite (Nuclear-Norm Regularization)**
We add a Frobenius-norm penalty every 50 steps to push weights toward low-rank structure, which makes GPTQ+Brotli compression more effective downstream. Full NuMuon with Block Krylov SVD is too expensive here, so this is a cheaper proxy that gets most of the benefit.
